description: Per-screenshot allowlist for the US-158 image pipeline. Each entry pairs a stable logical 'id' (used as the canonical reference from <Screenshot id='…' /> in MDX and from src/sections/* in React) with an absolute-from-dev-repo-root 'source' path. The sync-content.mjs handleImageAllowlist transform reads this file, validates every source resolves under the sync-allowlist.json 'screenshots-final' boundary, runs Sharp to produce AVIF / WebP / PNG triplets plus a 400w WebP thumbnail per entry, and writes public/screenshots/manifest.json mapping id -> { avif, webp, png, thumb, width, height, alt, priority }. The per-asset 80 KB AVIF budget is enforced; a regression fails the build. NOTE: chapter directory names use a mix of em-dash + non-breaking space (U+00A0) and em-dash + regular space — preserve byte-exact source paths or sync-content.mjs will ENOENT.

screenshots.alt: FramePath's hero view on Mac — screenplay on the left, scene navigator in the middle, shot list on the right.
Write stage on Mac — the element editor on the left with the Show Script inspector panel rendering the formatted screenplay alongside.
Plan stage on Mac — the shot list grid showing the full column layout with status, shot number, board, production fields, headline, and description.
Storyboard board view on Mac — hand-drawn frames arranged in shot order across the scene.
Shoot Mode on iPad — the shot list with enlarged status circles and storyboard thumbnails, ready for set.
